reasoning: To win the NFL Offensive Rookie of the Year award, Luther Burden would need to first be drafted (likely in the first round), land in a favorable situation, and outperform other highly-touted prospects. Typical favorites for the OROY award include top-drafted quarterbacks (e.g., Shedeur Sanders, Cam Ward), elite running backs (e.g., Ashton Jeanty), and other elite receivers (e.g., Travis Hunter, Tetairoa McMillan). Historically, individual non-quarterbacks entering the draft have low baseline chances (around 2-6%) of winning OROY before the draft has even occurred, given the volatility of draft position, injuries, landing spots, and quarterback competition. With Jeanty, Sanders, Ward, Hunter, and McMillan all presenting massive competition for the top rookie award in the upcoming 2025 class, Burden's chances are likely a slightly below-average top-tier WR prospect's odds, approximately 4%.
